thesis: Optical-fibre/telecom-infra demand and order wins are driving both a sharp price re-rating and strong upward analyst revisions; the dual signal is the cleanest in my universe.
falsification: Revisions stall or turn down while the stock goes sideways/falls for a quarter, signalling the move was a crowded blow-off rather than an earnings up-cycle.

thesis: Clean-energy and defense precision-components order book is lifting estimates; price and revision momentum agree.
falsification: Order-book or margin disappointment that triggers an estimate-cut and breaks the price trend.

thesis: Tanker/dry-bulk rate strength supports cash flows; rare combination of cheap valuation and rising analyst revisions.
falsification: Freight rates roll over and revisions turn negative, removing the value-plus-revision support.

thesis: Firm aluminium/alumina prices are driving upgrades; part of a deliberate metals tilt into the commodity up-cycle.
falsification: LME aluminium rolls over and revisions fade across the metals complex.

thesis: Rising derivatives volumes and product expansion are lifting estimates; structural volume compounder.
falsification: Volume growth stalls or a regulatory change hits the franchise and revisions turn down.

thesis: Transmission and grid-modernisation capex is driving a multi-year order up-cycle; estimates are rising.
falsification: Order inflow slows or margins compress, turning revisions negative.

thesis: Transformer demand from grid expansion and renewables is lifting both price and estimates.
falsification: Transformer order growth slows and revisions roll over.

thesis: Security-electronics demand and a recent listing tailwind are driving sharp upgrades.
falsification: Post-listing momentum fades and analysts cut estimates.

thesis: Defense and mining-explosives order book is lifting estimates strongly; durable structural demand.
falsification: Defense order momentum slows or revisions turn down.

thesis: Firm iron-ore realisations and volume growth are driving upgrades; part of the metals tilt.
falsification: Iron-ore prices fall and revisions across miners turn negative.

thesis: Global pipe order book and energy-infra spend are lifting estimates.
falsification: Pipe order inflow slows and revisions fade.

thesis: India EMS/electronics localisation is driving an estimate up-cycle.
falsification: EMS order wins slow or margins disappoint, cutting revisions.

thesis: Improving asset quality and earnings are driving upgrades from a cheap base.
falsification: Asset-quality slippage or NIM compression turns revisions negative.

thesis: Cash and derivatives volume growth plus data monetisation are lifting estimates.
falsification: Volume/market-share loss or regulatory drag turns revisions down.

thesis: Firm zinc prices and dividends are supporting upgrades.
falsification: Zinc prices roll over and revisions fade.

thesis: Housing and infra electrification demand is driving an estimate up-cycle.
falsification: Cable demand slows or copper-cost pass-through fails, cutting revisions.

thesis: Telecom-infra and defense-electronics order flow is driving strong price momentum.
falsification: Telecom capex pause or order slippage breaks the price trend.

thesis: Energy and infra project-management order book is lifting estimates from a cheap base.
falsification: Order inflow slows and revisions turn down.

thesis: Cement, VSF and the paints build-out are driving estimate upgrades.
falsification: Paints losses widen or core margins compress, cutting revisions.

thesis: Stable crude and production stability support upgrades from a very cheap valuation.
falsification: Crude falls sharply or windfall-tax/policy hit turns revisions negative.

thesis: Defense and export order book plus auto cycle are lifting estimates.
falsification: Export/auto demand softens and revisions roll over.

thesis: Domestic defense-electronics order book is driving estimate upgrades.
falsification: Order execution slips and revisions turn negative.

thesis: Diversified metals/resources with firm commodity prices and rising revisions; core metals holding.
falsification: Commodity prices roll over or a leverage/de-merger overhang turns revisions negative.

thesis: Diversified auto/aero/defense components with an order-driven estimate up-cycle.
falsification: Auto demand softens and revisions fade.

thesis: Power-equipment export and domestic capex order book is driving the estimate up-cycle.
falsification: Order inflow slows and revisions turn down.

thesis: Upstream aluminium plus Novelis recovery support rising estimates.
falsification: Aluminium prices or Novelis spreads roll over, cutting revisions.

thesis: Industrial decarbonisation and energy-efficiency capex is driving the order book.
falsification: Capex order inflow slows and revisions fade.

thesis: Capacity additions (thermal plus renewables) underpin steady earnings growth and upgrades.
falsification: Regulatory return cuts or renewable-execution delays turn revisions negative.

thesis: Strong domestic defense order book supports steady upgrades.
falsification: Order inflow slows or execution slips, turning revisions down.

thesis: Mining-consumables demand and pricing are driving estimate upgrades.
falsification: Mining-capex slows and revisions roll over.

thesis: Power-backup and industrial-engine demand support the order book.
falsification: Genset demand softens and momentum rolls over.

thesis: Capacity expansion plus firm steel spreads support earnings.
falsification: Steel spreads compress and revisions turn negative.

thesis: Data-centre and industrial power-gen demand supports the order book.
falsification: Power-gen demand softens and revisions fade.

thesis: Broad industrial demand supports steady growth.
falsification: Industrial demand softens and momentum/revisions roll over.

thesis: Multi-year aircraft and engine order book underpins earnings visibility.
falsification: Order-execution delays or a budget cut turn revisions negative.

thesis: Best-in-class private bank provides low-beta financials exposure and stability.
falsification: Asset-quality deterioration or NIM compression turns the earnings trend down.

thesis: Improving return ratios support a gradual re-rating.
falsification: Credit costs rise or growth slows, breaking the earnings trend.

thesis: Renewable-capacity build-out supports cash-flow growth.
falsification: Execution delays or tariff pressure turn revisions negative.
